datasets.name: kpi_total_holdings
datasets.query: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT holding_id) AS value FROM main.extraction_demo.model_holdings

datasets.name: kpi_flagged
datasets.query: SELECT COUNT(*) AS value FROM main.extraction_demo.model_assumptions WHERE approval_status = 'flagged'

datasets.name: kpi_assumption_driven
datasets.query: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT holding_id) AS value FROM main.extraction_demo.model_bridges WHERE quarter = 'Q3 2025' AND ABS(assumption_component_mm) > ABS(operational_component_mm) AND total_change_mm != 0

datasets.name: kpi_latest_buyout_multiple
datasets.query: SELECT ROUND(avg_buyout_ebitda_multiple, 2) AS value FROM main.extraction_demo.model_drift ORDER BY CONCAT(SUBSTR(quarter, 4, 4), '-Q', SUBSTR(quarter, 2, 1)) DESC LIMIT 1

datasets.name: holdings_by_strategy
datasets.query: SELECT strategy, COUNT(*) AS holdings FROM main.extraction_demo.model_holdings GROUP BY strategy ORDER BY holdings DESC

datasets.name: method_distribution
datasets.query: SELECT method, COUNT(*) AS holdings FROM main.extraction_demo.model_assumptions WHERE quarter = 'Q3 2025' GROUP BY method ORDER BY holdings DESC

datasets.name: drift_buyout
datasets.query: SELECT CONCAT(SUBSTR(quarter, 4, 4), '-Q', SUBSTR(quarter, 2, 1)) AS quarter_sort, quarter AS quarter_label, ROUND(avg_buyout_ebitda_multiple, 2) AS avg_multiple, ROUND(median_buyout_ebitda_multiple, 2) AS median_multiple FROM main.extraction_demo.model_drift WHERE avg_buyout_ebitda_multiple IS NOT NULL ORDER BY quarter_sort

datasets.name: drift_geq
datasets.query: SELECT CONCAT(SUBSTR(quarter, 4, 4), '-Q', SUBSTR(quarter, 2, 1)) AS quarter_sort, quarter AS quarter_label, ROUND(avg_geq_revenue_multiple, 2) AS avg_multiple, ROUND(median_geq_revenue_multiple, 2) AS median_multiple FROM main.extraction_demo.model_drift WHERE avg_geq_revenue_multiple IS NOT NULL ORDER BY quarter_sort

datasets.name: drift_infra
datasets.query: SELECT CONCAT(SUBSTR(quarter, 4, 4), '-Q', SUBSTR(quarter, 2, 1)) AS quarter_sort, quarter AS quarter_label, ROUND(avg_infra_discount_rate * 100, 2) AS avg_rate_pct, ROUND(median_infra_discount_rate * 100, 2) AS median_rate_pct FROM main.extraction_demo.model_drift WHERE avg_infra_discount_rate IS NOT NULL ORDER BY quarter_sort

datasets.name: drift_re
datasets.query: SELECT CONCAT(SUBSTR(quarter, 4, 4), '-Q', SUBSTR(quarter, 2, 1)) AS quarter_sort, quarter AS quarter_label, ROUND(avg_re_cap_rate * 100, 2) AS avg_rate_pct, ROUND(median_re_cap_rate * 100, 2) AS median_rate_pct FROM main.extraction_demo.model_drift WHERE avg_re_cap_rate IS NOT NULL ORDER BY quarter_sort

datasets.name: bridge_stacked
datasets.displayName: Bridge Attribution by Fund (Latest Q, stacked)
datasets.query: SELECT fund_id, 'Operational' AS component, ROUND(SUM(operational_component_mm), 1) AS value_mm FROM main.extraction_demo.model_bridges WHERE quarter = 'Q3 2025' GROUP BY fund_id UNION ALL SELECT fund_id, 'Assumption' AS component, ROUND(SUM(assumption_component_mm), 1) AS value_mm FROM main.extraction_demo.model_bridges WHERE quarter = 'Q3 2025' GROUP BY fund_id UNION ALL SELECT fund_id, 'Methodology' AS component, ROUND(SUM(methodology_component_mm), 1) AS value_mm FROM main.extraction_demo.model_bridges WHERE quarter = 'Q3 2025' GROUP BY fund_id ORDER BY fund_id, component

datasets.name: bridge_with_assumptions
datasets.query: SELECT b.fund_id, h.company_name, h.sector, a_curr.method, CASE a_curr.method WHEN 'comparable_multiples' THEN COALESCE(ROUND(a_prev.ebitda_multiple, 2), ROUND(a_prev.revenue_multiple, 2)) WHEN 'dcf' THEN ROUND(a_prev.discount_rate * 100, 2) WHEN 'cap_rate' THEN ROUND(a_prev.cap_rate * 100, 2) ELSE NULL END AS prev_assumption, CASE a_curr.method WHEN 'comparable_multiples' THEN COALESCE(ROUND(a_curr.ebitda_multiple, 2), ROUND(a_curr.revenue_multiple, 2)) WHEN 'dcf' THEN ROUND(a_curr.discount_rate * 100, 2) WHEN 'cap_rate' THEN ROUND(a_curr.cap_rate * 100, 2) ELSE NULL END AS curr_assumption, CASE a_curr.method WHEN 'dcf' THEN 'pct' WHEN 'cap_rate' THEN 'pct' ELSE 'x' END AS assumption_unit, ROUND(b.operational_component_mm, 1) AS operational_mm, ROUND(b.assumption_component_mm, 1) AS assumption_mm, ROUND(b.closing_fair_value_mm, 1) AS closing_mm, ROUND(b.total_change_mm, 1) AS total_change_mm FROM main.extraction_demo.model_bridges b JOIN main.extraction_demo.model_holdings h ON b.holding_id = h.holding_id JOIN main.extraction_demo.model_assumptions a_curr ON b.holding_id = a_curr.holding_id AND b.quarter = a_curr.quarter JOIN main.extraction_demo.model_assumptions a_prev ON b.holding_id = a_prev.holding_id AND b.prev_quarter = a_prev.quarter WHERE b.quarter = 'Q3 2025' ORDER BY ABS(b.assumption_component_mm) DESC

datasets.name: cross_gp_divergence
datasets.query: SELECT a.fund_id, ROUND(AVG(a.ebitda_multiple), 2) AS avg_ebitda_multiple, COUNT(*) AS holdings FROM main.extraction_demo.model_assumptions a JOIN main.extraction_demo.model_holdings h ON a.holding_id = h.holding_id WHERE h.sector = 'Media & Entertainment' AND a.quarter = 'Q3 2025' AND a.ebitda_multiple IS NOT NULL GROUP BY a.fund_id ORDER BY avg_ebitda_multiple

datasets.name: flagged_assumptions
datasets.query: SELECT a.fund_id, a.quarter, h.company_name, h.sector, a.method, CASE a.method WHEN 'comparable_multiples' THEN COALESCE(ROUND(a.ebitda_multiple, 2), ROUND(a.revenue_multiple, 2)) WHEN 'dcf' THEN ROUND(a.discount_rate * 100, 2) WHEN 'cap_rate' THEN ROUND(a.cap_rate * 100, 2) ELSE NULL END AS assumption_value, CASE a.method WHEN 'dcf' THEN 'pct' WHEN 'cap_rate' THEN 'pct' ELSE 'x' END AS unit, a.assumption_source, a.approval_status, SUBSTRING(a.notes, 1, 200) AS notes FROM main.extraction_demo.model_assumptions a JOIN main.extraction_demo.model_holdings h ON a.holding_id = h.holding_id WHERE a.approval_status = 'flagged' ORDER BY a.fund_id, CONCAT(SUBSTR(a.quarter, 4, 4), '-Q', SUBSTR(a.quarter, 2, 1))

datasets.name: assumption_source_nav
datasets.displayName: NAV by Assumption Data Source (Latest Q)
datasets.query: WITH latest AS (SELECT * FROM main.extraction_demo.model_drift ORDER BY CONCAT(SUBSTR(quarter, 4, 4), '-Q', SUBSTR(quarter, 2, 1)) DESC LIMIT 1) SELECT 'Disclosed' AS assumption_source, ROUND(nav_disclosed_mm, 0) AS nav_mm FROM latest UNION ALL SELECT 'Back-calculated', ROUND(nav_back_calculated_mm, 0) FROM latest UNION ALL SELECT 'Extracted', ROUND(nav_extracted_mm, 0) FROM latest

datasets.name: data_access_registry
datasets.query: SELECT d.assumption_source, d.access_mechanism, d.fund_id, d.gp_name, COUNT(DISTINCT h.holding_id) AS holdings, ROUND(SUM(v.fair_value_mm), 1) AS nav_mm FROM main.extraction_demo.model_data_access d JOIN main.extraction_demo.model_holdings h ON d.fund_id = h.fund_id JOIN main.extraction_demo.model_valuations v ON h.holding_id = v.holding_id AND v.quarter = 'Q3 2025' GROUP BY d.assumption_source, d.access_mechanism, d.fund_id, d.gp_name ORDER BY d.assumption_source, d.fund_id

datasets.name: data_access_summary
datasets.query: SELECT d.assumption_source, d.access_mechanism, COUNT(DISTINCT d.fund_id) AS funds, COUNT(DISTINCT h.holding_id) AS holdings, ROUND(SUM(v.fair_value_mm), 0) AS nav_mm FROM main.extraction_demo.model_data_access d JOIN main.extraction_demo.model_holdings h ON d.fund_id = h.fund_id JOIN main.extraction_demo.model_valuations v ON h.holding_id = v.holding_id AND v.quarter = 'Q3 2025' GROUP BY d.assumption_source, d.access_mechanism ORDER BY d.assumption_source

widget.name: w_title
widget.textbox_spec:
# Mark-to-Model Governance

Fair value attribution across 69 holdings, 4 strategies, 7 quarters. Every NAV change decomposed into **operational** (underlying business performance) and **assumption** (comp set, discount rate, or cap rate changes). Assumptions are where governance risk hides.

widget.name: w_s1_header
widget.textbox_spec:
## 1. Portfolio Composition

How the portfolio splits by strategy and by valuation method. Different strategies use structurally different models — EBITDA multiples for buyout, revenue multiples for growth equity, DCF for infrastructure, cap rates for real estate.

widget.name: w_s2_header
widget.textbox_spec:
## 2. Assumption Drift Over Time

Are assumptions creeping? Each chart tracks the portfolio average and median for its strategy's key assumption. Systematic drift without explicit decisions is a governance failure — it means NAV is moving for reasons that were never approved.

frame.title: Buyout — EBITDA Multiple Over Time (Avg & Median)
Growth Equity — Revenue Multiple Over Time (Avg & Median)
Infrastructure — Discount Rate Over Time (Avg & Median, %)
Real Estate — Cap Rate Over Time (Avg & Median, %)

widget.name: w_s3_header
widget.textbox_spec:
## 3. Attribution Bridge — Q3 2025

Every NAV change split into operational (revenue, EBITDA, metrics changed) and assumption (comp set, discount rate, or cap rate changed). Left: stacked by fund. Right: holding-level detail showing the specific assumption change (prev → current) that drove it.

frame.title: Operational vs Assumption Change by Fund ($M) — Q3 2025
Holding-Level Bridge with Assumption Detail — Q3 2025 (sorted by assumption impact)

widget.name: w_s4_header
widget.textbox_spec:
## 4. Cross-GP Divergence

Two buyout funds holding Media & Entertainment assets in the same quarter, using peer comps from different vintage perspectives. BYT-2019-I uses conservative public-market comparables; BYT-2022-I uses growth-oriented peers. Same sector, structurally different assumptions — both technically defensible.

frame.title: Avg EBITDA Multiple — Media & Entertainment Holdings (Q3 2025)

widget.name: w_s5_header
widget.textbox_spec:
## 5. Governance Queue — Flagged Assumptions

Assumptions flagged for review across all quarters. Each row shows the specific assumption value, the method it applies to, and the rationale note. These are the items that require investment committee sign-off before the valuation can be used in LP reporting.

widget.name: w_s6_header
widget.textbox_spec:
## 6. Data Access Registry

How the LP obtains valuation assumptions for each fund. **Disclosed:** GP provides assumptions directly via LPAC reporting package or side letter provision. **Back-calculated:** LP derives implied multiples from reported fair value and operating metrics. **Extracted:** assumptions parsed from unstructured GP narrative letters via NLP. The mechanism determines confidence in the underlying data.

frame.title: Data Access by Fund — Source, Mechanism, and NAV Exposure
